findings.evidence: `rg` shows `TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Contractability.lean:134:private theorem map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw ... ((pathLaw μ X).map (fun x : ℕ → α => fun k => x (φ k))).map (prefixProj α n) = blockLaw μ X (fun i : Fin n => φ i.val)`, while the PR adds `TauCeti/Probability/PathSpace/Shift.lean:85:theorem map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw ... = blockLaw μ X (fun i : Fin m => i.val + n)`.
findings.fix: Promote/move `map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw` from `Exchangeability/Contractability.lean` to a reusable public location and prove this shifted lemma by specializing it with `φ := fun k => k + n` plus `shift_iterate_eq_reindex`, or expose only the general lemma if it suffices.
findings.issue: `map_prefixProj_shift_iterate_pathLaw` is only the `φ k = k + n` specialization of the existing arbitrary-reindexing path-law lemma, creating a parallel public API for a special case.

summary: The shifted-prefix law duplicates an existing arbitrary-reindexing path-law helper instead of exposing/reusing the general API. Promote that general lemma or move it to the new path-space file, then derive the shift-iterate statement as its specialization.
